evals.prompt: Add ID Capture to my Cordova app so I can scan passports, driver's licenses and national ID cards and read the holder's name and date of birth. Here's my entry file: EmptyApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes a complete ID Capture integration inside the deviceready handler: Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey), IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments and a FullDocumentScanner wrapped in IdCaptureScanner, new Scandit.IdCapture(settings) + object-literal listener (didCaptureId/didRejectId), Scandit.Camera.withSettings(Scandit.IdCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings()) with null guard, Scandit.DataCaptureView.forContext + view.connectToElement on a #data-capture-view div, and pause/resume lifecycle.

assertions.text: '-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
settings.acceptedDocuments is configured with document objects like Passport, DriverLicense, IdCard via the Scandit.* namespace (semantic)
the listener is a plain object literal with didCaptureId and didRejectId methods, not a class implementing an interface (semantic)
the IdCapture mode is attached to the context via context.setMode or context.addMode (semantic)
connectToElement( is called on the view (semantic)
Camera.withSettings(...) is guarded for the null return value before being used (semantic)
Setup checklist mentions cordova plugin add for the Scandit plugins (semantic)
settings.scannerType is NOT used in the final code (semantic)
Scandit.IdCaptureRegion.Any or another PascalCase IdCaptureRegion member is used, not the lowercase Flutter-style form (semantic)
the bootstrap is wrapped inside the deviceready handler, not run at module load (semantic)

evals.prompt: I only need to read the PDF417 barcode on the back of US driver's licenses — nothing else. Set up ID Capture for that in EmptyApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill accepts only DriverLicense for IdCaptureRegion.Us and uses a SingleSideScanner with only the barcode flag enabled, wrapped in IdCaptureScanner.

assertions.text: the SingleSideScanner enables only the barcode flag, i.e. SingleSideScanner(true, false, false) (semantic)
data from the barcode is read, either via capturedId.barcode or via the harmonized top-level fields (documentNumber, fullName, dateOfBirth, etc.) that the barcode populates (semantic)

evals.prompt: Set up ID Capture to scan passports and extract the MRZ data (full name, document number, date of expiry). Use EmptyApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill accepts Passport, uses a scanner that reads the MRZ, and reads fields from the captured id / mrz result. The MRZ result is accessed via capturedId.mrzResult (not capturedId.mrz, which is the Flutter name).

assertions.text: the scanner reads the MRZ, via FullDocumentScanner or a SingleSideScanner with machineReadableZone enabled (semantic)
capturedId.mrz is NOT used (the Flutter name) (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add ID Capture but anonymize sensitive data — I don't want document images or sensitive fields in the result. Use EmptyApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets IdCaptureSettings.anonymizationMode to an IdAnonymizationMode value that covers fields and images.

assertions.text: an anonymization mode covering fields and images is chosen, e.g. IdAnonymizationMode.FieldsAndImages (semantic)

evals.prompt: I need the cropped document image and the face image back from each scanned ID, and I want to render them in an tag. Set that up in EmptyApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables image extraction via setShouldPassImageTypeToResult for IdImageType.Face and IdImageType.CroppedDocument, reads capturedId.images, and renders the base64 strings via the data: URI form (since IdImages returns base64 strings on Cordova, not URIs or HTMLImageElements).

assertions.text: the cropped document image is read via images.getCroppedDocument(Scandit.IdSide.Front) — not a non-existent .croppedDocument getter (semantic)
the base64 image is rendered using the data:image/png;base64, URI form on an element, not via .buffer / .bytes (semantic)

evals.prompt: We want to reject voided / cancelled US driver's licenses during ID Capture. How do I enable that? My entry file is EmptyApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-cordova-datacapture-id-voided-detection plugin (via cordova plugin add), sets settings.rejectVoidedIds = true, and handles Scandit.RejectionReason.DocumentVoided in didRejectId.

assertions.text: the scandit-cordova-datacapture-id-voided-detection plugin is added via cordova plugin add (semantic)

evals.prompt: We scan European driving licenses and need the vehicle categories from the back of the card. Set up ID Capture for that in EmptyApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-cordova-datacapture-id-europe-driving-license plugin, sets settings.decodeBackOfEuropeanDrivingLicense = true with a scanner that reads the back, and reads capturedId.vizResult.drivingLicenseDetails.drivingLicenseCategories, using category.code (the documented getter), not the non-existent categoryCode.

assertions.text: each category's data is read via the .code getter, not a non-existent .categoryCode (semantic)
a scanner that reads the back of the card is used, e.g. FullDocumentScanner (semantic)
the scandit-cordova-datacapture-id-europe-driving-license plugin is added via cordova plugin add (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add forged-barcode detection for US driver's licenses (AAMVA) to my ID Capture setup so we can flag likely-forged IDs. Use EmptyApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-cordova-datacapture-id-aamva-barcode-verification plugin, sets settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true, and/or reads capturedId.verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification with Scandit.AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus. It does NOT use a standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier class (removed on Cordova at v8).

assertions.text: verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification is referenced, or RejectionReason.ForgedAamvaBarcode is handled (semantic)
Scandit.AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus is referenced when inspecting the result (semantic)
the scandit-cordova-datacapture-id-aamva-barcode-verification plugin is added via cordova plugin add (semantic)
a standalone Scandit.AamvaBarcodeVerifier class is NOT used in the code (semantic)

evals.prompt: How should I handle the case where a document is detected but can't be captured? I want to show the user a clear message. My entry file is EmptyApp.js and I accept passports and driver's licenses.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements didRejectId and maps Scandit.RejectionReason values (Timeout, NotAcceptedDocumentType, etc.) to user-facing messages, disabling capture while the message is shown.

assertions.text: rejection reasons are mapped to user-facing messages, e.g. via a switch on RejectionReason (semantic)
idCapture.isEnabled is set to false while the rejection message is shown (semantic)
